sources.title: A Year of MCP: From Internal Experiment to Industry Standard
claims_supported: 97M+ monthly SDK downloads as of March 2026
OpenAI adopted MCP in March 2025 across Agents SDK, Responses API, and ChatGPT desktop
Google DeepMind confirmed MCP support in Gemini in April 2025
MCP became the universal standard for connecting AI agents to enterprise tools

sources.title: Anthropic: Donating the Model Context Protocol and Establishing the Agentic AI Foundation
claims_supported: AAIF co-founded by Anthropic, Block, and OpenAI under Linux Foundation
Google, Microsoft, AWS, Cloudflare, and Bloomberg as AAIF supporters
Open governance model confirmed — MCP donated from Anthropic to AAIF
Timeline event: December 2025 governance milestone

sources.title: The State of MCP — Adoption, Security & Production Readiness
claims_supported: Command injection vulnerabilities affect 43% of tested MCP implementations
Over 1,800 MCP servers found on public internet without authentication
75% of MCP servers built by individuals with no centralized security review
PESTAL Legal: security surface and governance gaps

sources.title: MCP Adoption Statistics 2025
claims_supported: MCP server downloads: ~100,000 in November 2024 → 8 million by April 2025
5,800+ MCP servers and 300+ MCP clients as of mid-2025
Remote MCP servers up nearly 4x since May 2025
AI integration middleware market projected at $10.3B in 2025
28% of Fortune 500 deployed MCP servers (Q1 2025), up from 12% in 2024

sources.title: 2026: The Year for Enterprise-Ready MCP Adoption
claims_supported: 2026 declared the pivotal year for widespread enterprise MCP adoption
Shift from pilot projects to full-scale production AI deployments via MCP
Enterprise OAuth 2.1 roadmap for Q2 2026
PESTAL Economic: enterprise AI deployment cost reduction via MCP standardization

sources.title: Model Context Protocol Security: Critical Vulnerabilities Every CISO Must Address
claims_supported: OWASP ranks prompt injection as #1 LLM security risk — amplified in MCP ecosystems
CVE-2025-6514: unpatched installs exploitable as supply-chain backdoors
September 2025 Postmark MCP server supply chain attack (1,500 weekly downloads)
AI agent accountability gaps when actions taken via MCP tool calls

sources.title: One Year of Model Context Protocol: From Experiment to Industry Standard
claims_supported: MCP servers grew from 3 implementations (Nov 2024) to 6,878 (Nov 2025) — 2,200% growth in 13 months
MCP created by Anthropic engineers David Soria Parra and Justin Spahr-Summers
M×N problem definition and how MCP solves it
GitHub and Microsoft joined MCP steering committee in May 2025

sources.title: Why the Model Context Protocol Won
claims_supported: Unanimous support from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft triggered S-curve adoption
Difficult to name other technologies gaining such unanimous big-tech support simultaneously
MCP evolution from vendor-led spec to common infrastructure
PESTAL Social: developer community adoption speed and dynamics

sources.title: Google Launches Managed MCP Servers — Agent-Ready by Design
claims_supported: Google launched managed MCP servers on Google Cloud in December 2025
Google officially joining the MCP ecosystem beyond spec endorsement
Timeline event: December 2025 Google managed server launch

sources.title: Top 10 AI Trends 2025: How Agentic AI and MCP Changed IT
claims_supported: MCP named as a top AI trend of 2025 alongside agentic AI
IT organizations reorienting around MCP for enterprise AI integration
Gartner: 1,445% surge in multi-agent system inquiries Q1 2024 → Q2 2025
AI agents market: $7.84B (2025) → $52.62B (2030) at 46% CAGR

sources.title: MCP History: From Anthropic's Fragmentation Fix to AI Standard
claims_supported: Anthropic launched MCP in November 2024 with Python and TypeScript SDKs
MCP addressed the M×N integration problem in enterprise AI
Protocol uses client-server model over stdio or HTTP+SSE
Authentication mechanisms absent from early spec; OAuth added March 2025

sources.title: RedMonk: Fastest-ever S-curve protocol adoption for MCP
claims_supported: RedMonk calls MCP 'the fastest-ever protocol adoption' they have recorded
S-curve adoption pattern compared to Docker's rapid market saturation
Developer demand for MCP-native IDE tooling in agentic workflows
PESTAL Social: developer community adoption dynamics and velocity

unverified_claims.claim: Market share percentages for Anthropic (~35%), Microsoft (~30%), OpenAI (~25%), Google (~8%), Block/AWS (~3%)
unverified_claims.note: No analyst report provides specific market share figures for MCP ecosystem players. Percentages are estimated based on relative developer mindshare, enterprise deployment counts, and SDK download attribution. All marked [unverified] in trend data.
Derived from 'remote MCP servers up nearly 4x since May 2025' (MCP Manager) — a 9-month figure yielding roughly +75% per quarter. No direct Q4 2025 to Q1 2026 single-quarter data point available.

No direct keyword tool data queried. Estimate based on: (a) 20 most searched MCP servers generating 180,000+ monthly searches (MCP Stats), (b) overall developer ecosystem size, and (c) Google Trends showing explosive growth through 2025. Likely an undercount of total branded search volume.

unverified_claims.claim: Environmental PESTAL — net compute impact of MCP agent proliferation
unverified_claims.note: No published data on energy consumption attributable to MCP-enabled AI agent deployments. Assessment is qualitative and directional only.
